name: Regression/exception-handling
description: Server error-handling conformance ported from fhir-server's Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E ExceptionTests.cs. Only 2 of the 4 source test methods are portable to an arbitrary external target server: GivenAnUnknownRoute_WhenPostingToHttp_TheServerShouldReturnAnOperationOutcome (unmapped route -> 404) and GivenANotAllowedMethod_WhenRequestIsSent_TheServerShouldReturnMethodNotSupported (unsupported HTTP verb -> 405). SKIPPED: GivenAnInternalThrowQuerystring_WhenPostingToHttp_TheServerShouldReturnAnOperationOutcome and GivenAMiddlewareThrowQuerystring_WhenPostingToHttp_TheServerShouldReturnAnOperationOutcome both require the source test's in-process StartupWithThrowingMiddleware test harness (a custom ASP.NET Core pipeline that deliberately throws on '?throw=internal'/'?throw=middleware') which no deployed/external FHIR server exposes; there is no generic conformance behavior to assert here. The method-not-allowed case is also an approximation: the source test sends HEAD to a fixed in-proc static route ('admin.html') that does not exist on arbitrary FHIR servers, so this suite instead sends HEAD to a normal resource-type endpoint (Patient) and treats the outcome as informational (warningOnly) since HTTP method support for FHIR endpoints beyond GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH is not mandated by the FHIR spec.

test.name: Unknown route returns 404
test.description: GET {base}/unknownRoute (a path that matches no defined FHIR interaction or resource type route) must return HTTP 404 Not Found. Per FHIR R4 §2.1.0.9 (RESTful error handling) servers SHOULD return an OperationOutcome for error responses, so the OperationOutcome shape/issue checks are warningOnly: a route that is entirely outside the FHIR interaction model (never resolves to a resource type) may be handled by generic web-framework routing that returns a plain 404 without a FHIR body.

operation.description: GET /unknownRoute — a path that does not correspond to any FHIR resource type or interaction

assert.description: Server must return HTTP 404 Not Found
Response body should be an OperationOutcome (warningOnly: routing-level 404s may bypass FHIR error formatting)
OperationOutcome should contain an error-severity issue (warningOnly, see above)
Issue code should be 'not-found' per FHIR IssueType (warningOnly: source E2E test asserts OperationOutcome.IssueType.NotFound, but the exact code used for routing-level errors varies by server)

test.name: Unsupported HTTP method against a resource endpoint (approximated)
test.description: Source test GivenANotAllowedMethod_WhenRequestIsSent_TheServerShouldReturnMethodNotSupported sends HEAD to a fixed static in-proc route ('admin.html') and hard-asserts 405. That route does not exist on arbitrary FHIR servers, so this suite instead sends HEAD to Patient (a real resource-type endpoint). Both possible outcomes are informational: 405 Method Not Allowed (HEAD is not a standard FHIR interaction) or 200 (some servers alias HEAD to GET, which is also valid HTTP semantics).

operation.description: HEAD /Patient — HEAD is not one of the standard FHIR RESTful interactions

assert.description: Alternative A: server rejects HEAD with 405 Method Not Allowed (informational: not a FHIR spec requirement)
Alternative B: server aliases HEAD to GET and returns 200 (informational: also acceptable)
